---
title: "Hands-on: Software Containers"
format:
  html:
    toc-depth: 4
execute:
  eval: false
---

# Exercise

-   Inspect the `Dockerfile`in the workshop repository.
- Try to understand the effects of every line.
- Consider the following question: 
    - What happens if you install a library manually within the running container environment? Which of the elements (Container, Image, Recipe) is affected? What are the implications for computational reproducibility?

## Optional / advanced

If you work locally on your machine – not in the Github Codespace environment – you can experiment with the Dockerfile: add an R package to the recipe, change the exposed port, or add an entire new service. 


# Additional Resources

-   Docker documentation: <https://docs.docker.com/get-started/>
-   Rocker project: <https://rocker-project.org/>
-   CRS Primer on Software Containers: <https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.13757230>
-   The Turing Way — Reproducible Environments: <https://the-turing-way.netlify.app/reproducible-research/renv>
